highlights.text: I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: ES needs to lose 6832-29.” We lost it this morning, and flushed all day.
Readers know ES behaves in a highly predictable manner, alternating between vertical “elevator down” flushes, and equally violent squeezes.
The squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which is when price flushes a low, traps shorts, recovers it, squeezes.
This was a rapid, elevator down sell.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss the distinction between a Failed Breakdown (my core setup) and its complex sibling (the Level Reclaim).

post_title: CPI Tomorrow For SPX. Can It End The Slide Lower? Dec 18 Plan
